Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (arson â wildfires and places of worship)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (arson â wildfires and places of worship)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to create an offence for causing a wildfire and an offence for causing damage by fire or explosion to a place of worship. It also requires a court to consider as aggravating circumstances, in the context of arson by negligence, the fact that it resulted in a wildfire or the destruction of a place of worship.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 7(2.01) of the Criminal Code before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Offences in relation to cultural property
Subsection.Text: Despite anything in this Act or any other Act, a person who commits an act or omission outside Canada that if committed in Canada would constitute an offence under section 322, 341, 344, 380, 430, 434 or 435.2 in relation to cultural property as defined in Article 1 of the Convention, or a conspiracy or an attempt to commit such an offence, or being an accessory after the fact or counselling in relation to such an offence, is deemed to have committed that act or omission in Canada if the person

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (lxxix):
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 435:

Subsection.Text: Every person who, without lawful authority, intentionally causes a wildfire, is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for a first offence, for a term of not more than 14 years, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for each subsequent offence, for life; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for a first offence, of not more than $100,000, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for each subsequent offence, of not more than $250,000.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, wildfire means an uncontrolled fire in a forest or grassland.
Every person who, without lawful authority, intentionally causes damage by fire or explosion to a place of worship is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for a first offence, imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of five years, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for each subsequent offence, imprisonment for life and to a minimum punishment for a term of seven years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, place of worship means a building or structure, or part of a building or structure, that is primarily used for religious worship, including a church, mosque, synagogue or temple.

Section.Text: Section 436 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: If a person is convicted of an offence under this section, the court that imposes the sentence shall consider as an aggravating circumstance the fact that the fire or explosion resulted in a wildfire, as defined in subsection 435.1(2), or the destruction of a place of worship, as defined in subsection 435.2(2), and shall cause to be stated in the record how it took that fact into account when determining the sentence.
